content.claim: Hungary's primary school network contracted from above 6,000 schools to nearly 4,200 in a 10-year period (1960s–70s) under Communist-era rationalization policy, establishing historical precedent for top-down network consolidation independent of demographic demand alone.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Specific institutional sources not cited in research notes
Mid-20th-century policy context differs from contemporary governance structure
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
